title: Time Series Storage & Query Engine
short_description: High-performance columnar storage with compression (LZ4/zstd), time-based indexing, and FastAPI REST interface
long_description: High-performance columnar storage with compression (LZ4/zstd), time-based indexing, and FastAPI REST interface
technical_details:
## Algorithm & Strategy Structure

A columnar time-series database optimized for financial data access patterns:

**Storage engine**:
- **Columnar layout**: Each column (OHLCV) stored as a separate compressed array. Enables reading only the columns needed for a query.
- **Block encoding**: Fixed-size blocks of 4096 rows; each block stores min/max for efficient range-scan pruning.
- **Compression**: LZ4 for hot data (fast decompression, ~5µs/block), zstd for cold data (higher compression ratio, ~50µs/block).
- **Time-based indexing**: Timestamps stored as nanoseconds-since-epoch int64; binary search for range queries.

**FastAPI REST interface**:
```
GET /series/{symbol}?start=2024-01-01&end=2024-12-31&columns=close,volume
```
Returns Arrow IPC format for zero-copy deserialization in Python clients.

## Write Path

New data appended to a write-ahead log (WAL) for durability, then compacted into sorted columnar blocks. Background compaction merges small blocks.

## Query Performance

Benchmarked query types:
- Point lookup (single timestamp): < 100µs
- Range scan (1 year, daily): < 5ms
- Aggregation (monthly OHLCV from daily): < 20ms

## Metric Methodology

- **Read throughput**: MB/s for bulk range scans.
- **Compression ratio**: Compressed size / raw size, measured per asset type.
- **Query latency P99**: 99th percentile latency for range queries under simulated load (100 concurrent clients).
- **Write throughput**: Records/second for streaming tick data ingestion.
